rulings.Case Title: ALBERTO GONZALES VS. VERONICA SERMENO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 17, 2022 line 1. PLAINTIFF ALBERTO GONZALES MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD is OFF CALENDAR. No proof of service on file as of March 16, 2022 at 9:25 a.m. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: FOLSOM STREET INVESTMENTS, LLC ET AL VS. MICHAEL B. ROTHENBERG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 17, 2022 line 2. PLAINTIFF CRAIG HORDLOW APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER is continued to March 23, 2022 for parties to submit supplemental declarations including calculations for the following: (1) compound interest calculation accounting for 60,000 payments credited at the time when each payment was received (applied first to accrued interest; balance, if any, applied to principal); (2) compound interest calculations accounting for 68,000 credited at the time when each payment was purportedly made (applied first to accrued interest; balance, if any, applied to principal); (3) simple interest calculation accounting for 60,000 payments credited at the time when each payment was received (applied first to accrued interest; balance, if any, applied to principal); (4) simple interest calculations accounting for 68,000 credited at the time when each payment was purportedly made (applied first to accrued interest; balance, if any, applied to principal). Supplemental declarations limited to calculations listed above to be filed and served no later than March 18, 2022. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JIMMY CHERRY JR VS. WINDY R BRYANT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Requiring An Undertaking; Stay Of Proceedings; Preliminary Injunction; Order Reclassifying Case; And/Or Order Consolidating Cases
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 17, 2022 line 5. DEFENDANT WINDY CHERRY Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Requiring An Undertaking; Stay Of Proceedings; Preliminary Injunction; Order Reclassifying Case; And/Or Order Consolidating Cases is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Request for an undertaking is denied. Moving party failed to carry its burden under CCP 1030(a) and show its entitlement to attorney's fees per statute or contract and failed to establish by affidavit the nature and amount of the costs. Request to consolidate is granted. This action is consolidated for trial and discovery purposes with action CGC-21-594979. Motion to stay is granted, the consolidated matter 594979/668280 is stayed pending the resolution of probate matter in Alameda. During the pendency of the stay of the UD action moving party shall post reasonable rental value in the amount of $5,000 a month with the Court on the first of each month (or if the first of the month falls on a legal holiday/weekend, the next business day); defendant shall post $2,500 representing fair rental value for the 2 weeks of March 2022 no later than April 1, 2022. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JIMMY CHERRY JR VS. WINDY R BRYANT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set 3; Deeming Matters Specified In Requests For Admission, Set 2 Admitted; And Imposing Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 17, 2022 line 5. DEFENDANT WINDY CHERRY Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set 3; Deeming Matters Specified In Requests For Admission, Set 2 Admitted; And Imposing Monetary Sanctions is DENIED without prejudice. As Defendant correctly points out complicated issues of title are not appropriate for determination on an expedited unlawful detainer timeline, including discovery. Upon the expiration of the stay, if necessary and proper in light of the resolution or probate matter, Defendant may re-serve its discovery on a civil case timeline. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JIMMY CHERRY JR VS. WINDY R BRYANT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 17, 2022 line 5. PLAINTIFF JIMMY CHERRY, DELPHINE CHERRY Motion For Protective Order is DENIED without prejudice to be raised if necessary and proper should any arguments raised in this motion remain relevant to any subsequently propounded discovery upon the expiration of the stay. The Court will note that based on the nature and magnitude of the discovery dispute in this action, in the absence of a stay, the Court would order the parties to engage a discovery referee. Parties and their counsel are encouraged to follow the spirit, and not merely the letter, of the discovery act and engage in meaningful good faith meet and confer efforts regarding their differences. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: JIMMY CHERRY JR VS. WINDY R BRYANT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 17, 2022 line 5. PLAINTIFF JIMMY CHERRY, DELPHINE CHERRY Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration is DENIED. Moving party fails to sustain its burden under CCP 1008. No authority is cited for the proposition that CCP 1008 is applicable where a motion has not been received resulting in failure to oppose. Defendant provides evidence that the motion was properly served and delivered to Plaintiff's counsel in time to present their opposition to the Court. It also appears based on the evidence provided by Plaintiff that no verified responses have been received by Defendant as of the time the motion was filed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
